Ross and his friends must launch their most daring plan yet to try to save themselves and the country from enemies both at home and abroad. Last-ever episode of the drama, starring Aidan Turner(n)

programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Matt Baker and Emma Willis(n)

programme.title: River Walks
programme.desc: Kirsten O'Brien takes a 15-mile walk along the River Tee, exploring the history of Upper Teesdale, meeting some of extraordinary people who call its banks home and taking a wild swim in its white waters. She begins her trek at South Gare near Redcar - a childhood playground for Kirsten and her brother - and then takes in High Force, Middleton and ancient Egglestone Abbey(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: Police from the UK and Uganda trace a woman who fled Britain after killing her daughter, and officers in London are on the hunt for a man wanted in Italy for committing rape and serious assault. Meanwhile in Yorkshire, police search for a criminal who used a light aircraft to smuggle cocaine(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Comedian Paul Merton investigates his family history. His mother's Irish parents died when she was very young, so Paul sets out to piece together the story of his grandfather's involvement in key events leading up to Irish independence in the 1920s, and learns that the true circumstances of his death were very different from the story that had been handed down. Elsewhere in London, Paul traces a great, great grandmother on his father's side of the family who was sent to prison for her part in an assault(n)

programme.title: Heartbreak Holiday
programme.desc: Documentary following 10 strangers united by heartbreak as they head off for the summer of a lifetime around the Greek islands. In the final episode, emotions run high as the life-changing holiday comes to an end. The girls decide to confront Luke, and Archie and Melissa challenge themselves at sea(n)

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Cherry Healey and a team of independent scientists conduct an experiment that puts everyday cosmetic and beauty products to the test. Cherry discovers that products such a moisturisers may not have the benefits we once thought, and reveals an easy fix for cellulite that actually works. The programme also looks at the psychology behind why we buy certain cosmetic and beauty products(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: A Matter of Life and Debt
programme.desc: In Bridge of Allan, Karen and her 75-year-old father go into business together combining the ancient craft of glassblowing and Scotland's famous tipple, creating an unique range of whisky gifts. With the aid of a £25,000 loan the business is continuing to keep a dying art alive. Plus, a transgender entrepreneur takes control of her future after struggling with employment(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle observe events as rare Amur tiger Yana goes into labour, meaning the Park's first cubs for nearly two decades are imminent. Kate also catches up on life in the gorilla house with the focus on silverback Kesho, while Jean Johansson discovers why coconuts are just the job for the dental health of Cape Porcupines(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a croissant factory in France that produces 336,000 pastries a day, and discovers how an 83-year-old strain of yeast is used to create the perfect flavour. Cherry Healey talks to a professor who specialises in exploring human senses about the perfect way to eat a croissant and visits a farm that produces concentrated butter that is 99.8 percent fat. Plus, Ruth Goodman is in Paris investigating the croissant's surprising Austrian origins and the role of bread in the French revolution(n)

programme.title: BBC News at 9
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: Equestrian
programme.desc: Action from Rotterdam, where British Olympic gold medallists Charlotte Dujardin, Scott Brash and Ben Maher were among those expected to be competing(n)

programme.title: The Muppets
programme.desc: Three fans of the famous puppet variety show discover a powerful businessman is going to knock down the Muppet theatre and drill for oil on the site. They track down frontman Kermit the frog and persuade him to reunite his co-stars, so they can put on one last performance to save the venue. Comedy, starring Jason Segel, Amy Adams and Chris Cooper, with songs by Bret McKenzie (Flight of the Conchords)(n)

credits.actor: Jason Segel, Amy Adams, Chris Cooper, songs by Bret McKenzie (Flight of the Conchords)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: Michael Buerk helps search for heirs to Alexander Gibb's substantial estate, while a surprising family secret is discovered while delving into the case of Robert Heavens(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers continue their culinary history of British food by exploring breakfast dishes, revealing how the morning meal keeps evolving(n)

programme.title: World's Sneakiest Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham features a host of animals that use deceptive tactics to find food. Some trick their prey, like the manipulative mantis which looks like a flower and killer whales which set traps using sound. Others cheat each other - sea otters demand ransoms after kidnapping each other's pups - and some, like African bird the drongo and monkeys, fool other species. In parks, meanwhile, grey squirrels are at war in stealing raids and it seems these animal liars, cheats and hustlers really will stop at nothing to get a meal(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Stephanie Connell and Charlie Ross head out from Sevenoaks to an auction at Folkestone, learning the story of the Kent cobnut harvest along the way(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the first leg of a journey from the coalfields of South Wales to the southernmost tip of Cornwall, travelling from Whitland, Carmarthenshire, to Swansea(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Science and technology experts Spencer Kelly, Dr Helen Lawal, Dallas Campbell, Ajay Tegala and Rory Cellan-Jones take on the general-knowledge champions, hoping to win a cash prize for their chosen charity. Hosted by Jeremy Vine(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four more treasured family heirlooms, and the memories they hold, back to life, tackling a radio that survived Dunkirk, a battered leather gaming case, a grandfather clock with a poignant history, and a pair of Viking statues(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's Summer Feasts
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain prepares an array of mouth-watering dishes that she will serve up to her loved ones at her own summer party feast, including a barbecued butterflied leg of lamb with sticky rhubarb glaze. She makes side dishes of kiwi and feta salad and edamame wild rice, as well as olive and rosemary bread shaped like a crown. For dessert, she goes all out with a magical princess torte cake and an ingenious raspberry ripple eclair, and creates a thirst-quenching drink in the form of an elderflower cooler(n)

programme.title: A Black and White Killing: The Case That Shook America
programme.desc: Part two of two. Mobeen Azhar seeks to understand the complications surrounding the lives and behaviour of both perpetrator Russell Courtier and victim Larnell Bruce, looking more closely into the nature and extent of European Kindred's influence on the wider community. He investigates the brainwashing effect that membership of Oregon's racist prison gangs can have on young men, before hearing the jury deliver its verdict on whether Courtier is a racist murderer(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: The second of two editions celebrating LGBT+ stand-up comedy at the BBC, showcasing the funniest moments from stars who have appeared on Live at the Apollo. This edition features sets from Tom Allen, Stephen K Amos, Jen Brister, Al Porter, Julian Clary, Suzi Ruffell and Gina Yashere(n)

programme.title: I Love
programme.desc: Animated duo Beavis and Butt-Head reflect on a year in which conspiracy theories appeared left, right and centre as The X Files hit TV screens, and Mr Blobby began his illustrious career on Noel's House Party. Boy band rivalry appeared as East 17 embarked on their ill-fated mission to give Take That a run for their money and John Wayne Bobbitt came to know the true meaning of loss(n)

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: Steve Wright narrates a selection of appearances and promo videos taken from long-running music show Top of the Pops during the 1990s, including performances by Spice Girls, Michael Jackson, Pulp, the Prodigy, Madonna, Blur and Take That. First broadcast on New Year's Eve 1999(n)

programme.title: Fosse/Verdon
programme.desc: As the high summer of 1973 ebbs away, Bob and girlfriend Ann Reinking hire a weekend house in the country to help comfort the recently bereaved Neil Simon. Also invited are Gwen and her boyfriend Ron. Ann is concerned that Gwen won't like her, but Bob says Gwen will be on her best behaviour for Neil. Gwen proves to be in buoyant mood, excited to announce a theatre has been secured for their project of Chicago, but Bob is hesitant claiming he has a lot of work on with the Lenny script. Thus, new tensions emerge. Sam Rockwell and Michelle Williams star(n)

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: Aspiring designers take on commercial interior challenges, as they compete to win a life changing contract. Presented by Fearne Cotton. In the first episode, the ten contestants are split into two teams and must redesign two show homes, working together to deliver a cohesive end product that impresses the judges and is accessible to prospective buyers(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher visits the Lake District to take a look at some of the jobs working animals do. He joins the Cockermouth Mountain Rescue team to see search-and-rescue dogs in action and meets 15-week-old Jura, a border collie pup learning the ropes from the older dogs. He then meets George Newton and his two Dales ponies, Charlie and George. They work the forests for timber as they're able to get to places that normal machinery can't. But Sean is surprised to find that the biggest job is being done by the smallest creatures - beetles(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The reds and blues go bargain hunting in Lincolnshire, with the help of experts Nick Hall and Gary Pe. Plus, presenter Charlie Ross learns about learns about the rules on finding treasure at the bottom of the garden(n)

programme.title: A Matter of Life and Debt
programme.desc: A woman who took out an ethical loan of £900,000 to set up and run an indoor urban skate-park in Corby, Northamptonshire, creating a safe place for young people to hang out(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers investigate an explosion that left two brothers dead, while the victim of a motor insurance scam is determined not to let the fraudsters get away with it(n)

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer brave the wild lakes of Essex in search of the mighty carp. They have the latest high-tech equipment to catch the biggest fish they can - and are fully prepared to stay all night if they have to. The pair discuss the true meaning of a mid-life crisis and decide to visit a beauty clinic to see whether there are any suitable treatments available to re-capture their youth. Bob reveals his secret recipe to make the perfect scrambled eggs - but it's not the only thing that shocks Paul(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Kate Humble hosts the panel show that tests the wildlife knowledge of team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke, with guests Martin Hughes-Games and Lindsey Chapman(n)

programme.title: Home Away from Home
programme.desc: A house-proud couple from Staines in Surrey swap home with complete strangers in Norfolk to try out a new way of life, and see whether they enjoy the experience. Presented by Gloria Hunniford(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: Michael Buerk follows the team who have taken on the case of John Powney, whose passion for music and vast record collection could lead to unexpected riches for his heirs(n)

programme.title: Emergency Rescue Down Under
programme.desc: A British flying doctor swoops to the rescue after a little boy becomes trapped in machinery and two UK policemen race to catch a dangerous driver on the beach(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore Britain's knack for gardening as they continue their culinary journey through time. The duo also look at the country's passion for growing vegetables at home, a skill that has inspired some amazing dishes(n)

programme.title: World's Sneakiest Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham features animals that utilise devious tactics to win a mate and raise a family, such as bower birds and fiddler crabs, who use optical illusions to impress a female, and male topi antelope, who fake danger to give them an edge. He also looks at family life, including the chicks that blackmail their parents, and the honeyguide bird that dupes another species into raising its young(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Road Trip debutant and dealer Stephanie Connell and auctioneer Charlie Ross travel around Kent looking for items of interest, before heading to an auction in Greenwich, London(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Katharine Merry, Derek Redmond, Ryan Sidebottom, Paul Robinson and Peter Shilton form another celebrity team aiming to triumph over the Eggheads. Presented by Jeremy Vine(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Pontyclun to Ebbw Vale in Wales. Along the way, he visits the Royal Mint in Llantrisant and Cardiff Arms Park, scene of the first Welsh victory over the All Blacks back in 1905. In Cardiff Bay, Michael discovers the departure point for one of the Edwardian era's most famous expeditions - Scott's ill-fated voyage to the Antarctic(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restores a bass guitar that once topped the charts in the hands of Tony Wilson, founding member of 1970s funk legends Hot Chocolate, but has since fallen into disrepair. Other items include a vintage end-of-the-pier machine, a carved wooden plaque, and a hand-carved belt that marked an unlikely wartime friendship(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: RNLI volunteers in Pembrokeshire, including brothers Hal, Will and Ben, race to the scene when a plane makes an emergency crash landing on one of Wales' busiest beaches. In Jersey, the St Catherine crew are pushed to the brink when they search for a man who has gone missing at sea in the middle of a fierce winter storm. On the Wirral, the crew of Hoylake launch their specialist rescue hovercraft and race the incoming tide to try and save a horse, which has become stuck fast in the mud and at risk from drowning(n)

programme.title: The Chefs' Brigade
programme.desc: Jason Atherton takes his team to the Alpine region of the Savoie in France, where Michelin star chef Alain Perillat-Mercerot has prepared a seven-course menu to test their skills. One member of the brigade decides to leave, forcing the others to tackle the challenge short-handed. Jason teaches them how to make Quenelle de Brochet, using pike caught from a nearby lake(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Maisie Adam, Ed Byrne, Larry Dean, Ed Gamble, Tiff Stevenson and regular panellist Hugh Dennis. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Kirsty Wark presents analysis of the day's events(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: The Funeral Murders
programme.desc: Vanessa Engle's documentary examining the deadly series of events connecting two funerals in Belfast in March 1988, hearing from Republicans, Loyalists, security forces and victims' families. The chain began at the funeral of three IRA members shot by the SAS in Gibraltar, when loyalist paramilitary Michael Stone killed three mourners, including IRA member Kevin Brady, and injured 60 others. At Brady's funeral three days later, two British Army corporals drove into the cortege and were beaten and killed by some members of the crowd(n)

programme.title: Searching for Shergar
programme.desc: The story of one of the world's most valuable racehorses, Shergar, which was famously abducted from its stables in 1983, at the height of the Troubles, and never seen again. Thirty five years on, Alison Millar sets out to see if changed times will help her unearth the mystery once and for all(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate more potentially valuable works of art. They begin with an 18th-century landscape that could be a lost work by of one of the biggest names in British art, Thomas Gainsborough. The painting has been in the family of owner Mark Cropper for generations and until the 1970s it was considered to be a Gainsborough. But then a valuation downgraded it and Mark's father tore off the Gainsborough label in disgust. Now Mark would love to be able to re-apply the Gainsborough plaque(n)

programme.title: Can You Beat the Bookies?
programme.desc: Comedian and football presenter Lloyd Griffith embarks on an experiment to find out if he can uncover the secrets of gambling success and the truth about the industry(n)

programme.title: One Day That Changed My Life
programme.desc: A self-taught cook vies for first place in Gok Wan's restaurant awards and a financial director tries to combat his phobia of heights to take a new job in the UK's tallest building(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction(n)

programme.title: A Matter of Life and Debt
programme.desc: A dairy farmer in Darlington branches out, using a loan to obtain equipment to produce goats' milk and ice cream, while also running yoga classes in which the animals are involved(n)

programme.title: Animal Babies: First Year on Earth
programme.desc: Cameras follow six baby animals as they face the challenges of surviving their first year on Earth. In the final episode of the series, the six baby animals approach the end of their first year on Earth. In Iceland, an Arctic fox cub rejected by his mother must learn where to find food for the first time, while in Kenya, spotted hyena twin sisters must roam a territory they share with the biggest threat to their lives, lions. Whether breaking ties or building bonds, all the young animals must learn for themselves how to stay healthy and safe(n)

programme.title: BBC News at 9
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke are joined by Steve Leonard and Christy Harrison for another test of wildlife knowledge, hosted by Kate Humble(n)

programme.title: Home Away from Home
programme.desc: Families from Ascot and Sunderland put their homes and lifestyles under the microscope as they exchange properties with complete strangers. Presented by Gloria Hunniford(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: Michael Buerk and the team locate an heir living in one of the most famous addresses in the world, and also track down family members who could inherit a substantial windfall(n)

programme.title: Emergency Rescue Down Under
programme.desc: A British chopper pilot rushes to the aid of an elderly farmer who is fighting for his life, while two UK policemen discover a darker side to life down under(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore food-related customs associated with wedding ceremonies. The duo help prepare a Scottish breakfast fit for a bride and groom, and highlight traditions behind the star of the nuptial feast - the cake(n)

programme.title: Tigers About the House
programme.desc: Documentary following zookeeper Giles Clark as he hand-rears Sumatran tiger cubs Spot and Stripe, which were born at the Australia Zoo in Queensland and are one of the world's most critically endangered species. To help give them the best start and ensure their survival, Giles takes them home to live with his boisterous family and gives them round-the-clock care for the first four months of their lives. The film gives an intimate look into the crucial early development of the cubs as they struggle through their first crucial weeks, while Giles embarks upon the toughest parenting job of his life and tries to save the species from the brink of extinction in the wild. Andrew Lincoln narrates(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Stephanie Connell and Charlie Ross journey from Dunstable to the Cotswolds, where Charlie comes across an ancient naval relic, but Stephanie finds something even rustier(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: A team from the world of arts and entertainment comprising actor Simon Greenall, singer Toyah Willcox, arts correspondent Nick Higham, award-winning saxophonist YolanDa Brown and Proms presenter Katie Derham set out to triumph over the general knowledge Goliaths and win the cash prize for charity. Presented by Jeremy Vine(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo arrives in Newport, where he learns about the heroism of a young boy who was awarded the Albert Medal, before visiting a cinema dating from 1912 in Clevedon(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four family heirlooms back to life. Dominic Chinea undertakes the challenging restoration of a Carrom board, a traditional Asian board game, while Steven Kember teams up with silversmith Brenton West to repair a precious silver menorah. Elsewhere, Steve Fletcher restores a small wooden duck once used in magic tricks, and bookbinder Christopher Shaw repairs a pair of photo albums charting Captain Scott's Antarctic expedition(n)

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The contestants are split into pairs and challenged to transform shop interiors, revamping a series of independent stores on a high street in Nottingham in just two days. In a vintage store, the designers go right back to basics, stripping the very high walls and ceiling, while frustrations mount in a women's clothing store as last-minute changes are made to the design plans. With the shop makeovers complete, Michelle Ogundehin and guest judge Mary Portas decide who has impressed, and who will be leaving the competition(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.sub-title: Cannabis: Miracle Medicine or Dangerous Drug?
programme.desc: Horizon investigates the very latest medical and scientific research into the effects of cannabis on the brain and the body, with the medicinal form of the drug now legal. A&E doctor Javid Abdelmoneim wants to find out whether it will help or harm patients. He meets the young epilepsy patient responsible for changing the law around medicinal cannabis in the UK, and sees the remarkable effect it has on his condition. He visits a medicinal cannabis farm in Denmark to learn how a company known for growing the recreational drug are now producing medicinal cannabis to be exported all over Europe. He also travels to Israel, hoping to find out why they have been using cannabis as a medicine for more than 20 years, and meets scientists studying the safety and effectiveness of cannabis in treating pain(n)

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: After the electricity goes out in Sam's home, Sunny's ex-husband Jeff suggests that she get a generator in case the power fails again. He drives her to the DIY store to buy one, and she ends up running into Robin and his daughter. On the way home, Jeff tries to kiss Sam, but she rejects him. Comedy-drama, created by Pamela Adlon, starring Olivia Edward, Hannah Alligood, Mikey Madison and Pamela Adlon(n)

credits.actor: Olivia Edward, Hannah Alligood, Mikey Madison, Pamela Adlon

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits the capital city of Denmark, Copenhagen, where he is inspired to cook fried pork belly with lovage potatoes and parsley sauce - a popular Danish dish(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Kirsty Wark presents analysis of the day's events(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: The Chefs' Brigade
programme.desc: Jason Atherton takes his team to the Alpine region of the Savoie in France, where Michelin star chef Alain Perillat-Mercerot has prepared a seven course menu to test their skills. One member of the brigade decides to leave, forcing the others to tackle the challenge short-handed. Jason teaches them how to make Quenelle de Brochet, using pike caught from a nearby lake(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Building Cars: Secrets of the Assembly Line
programme.desc: IPart one of two. James May, Kate Humble and Ant Anstead broadcast from inside Mini Plant Oxford, one of Britain's biggest and busiest car factories, where a new car drives off the production line every 68 seconds. James looks at the use of robotic technology and investigates the claim that British manufacturing is dead, while Ant explores the history and heritage of one of the most iconic cars ever built, and Kate reports on the use of electric cars in Norway(n)

programme.title: Victoria & Albert: The Royal Wedding
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley oversees a restaging of the wedding of Queen Victoria and Prince Albert. Aided by food historian Annie Gray, clothing expert Harriet Waterhouse and military historian Jasdeep Singh, she recreates the most important elements of the ceremony and the celebrations, scouring history books, archives, newspapers and Victoria's diaries for the exact details of the couple's big day in 1840. Lucy also recounts the story of Victoria and Albert's courtship and engagement, exploring the hidden iconography and symbolism of this hugely significant wedding and revealing new insights into the pair's relationship, both public and private(n)

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: The focus turns to apes and monkeys, revealing their similarities to humans. Large armies of hamadryas baboons battle on the plains of Ethiopia to steal females and settle old scores, while Japanese macaques beat the cold by lounging in thermal springs. An orang-utan baby fails in its struggle to make an umbrella out of leaves, and capuchins try to smash nuts with a large rock. Narrated by David Attenborough(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: The Shelbys are lured by the Italians into a cat-and-mouse chase on the street of Birmingham, where it becomes clear that Tommy has met his match. Tommy tries to console himself with a visit from an old flame, but it soon becomes clear that he cannot always get what he wants. As his factory lies idle, Tommy confronts the possibility that the Communists might win and he'll be deemed a traitor to his class. Meanwhile, Changretta prepares to spring another trap. Period gangster drama, starring Cillian Murphy(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Tommy finds himself engaged in bloody battle with Luca Changretta and his gang. The family gather to find out what happened, but Lizzie has even greater news to break. Meanwhile, an army colonel has questions for Ada about her past as a Communist, and Jessie Eden confirms just how far she is prepared to go in pursuit of her cause. Sensing an opportunity to capitalise on his situation, Luca Changretta makes his way to London to present a plan to Alfie Solomons. Starring Cillian Murphy, Helen McCrory and Adrien Brody(n)

credits.actor: Cillian Murphy, Helen McCrory, Adrien Brody

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: The night of the big fight arrives, and as the bell rings and the crowd goes wild, dangers lurk in the shadows for Tommy Shelby and his family, and Tommy soon discovers who he can truly trust. Meanwhile, as Changretta plays his final ace, he sets in motion a series of events that will change the Peaky Blinders forever. Starring Cillian Murphy, Adrien Brody, Tom Hardy, Helen McCrory, Charlie Murphy, Sophie Rundle, Paul Anderson, Kate Phillips, Ned Dennehy and Jack Rowan(n)

credits.actor: Cillian Murphy, Adrien Brody, Tom Hardy, Helen McCrory, Charlie Murphy, Sophie Rundle, Paul Anderson, Kate Phillips, Ned Dennehy, Jack Rowan

programme.title: Victoria & Albert: The Royal Wedding

Aided by food historian Annie Gray, clothing expert Harriet Waterhouse and military historian Jasdeep Singh, she recreates the most important elements of the ceremony and the celebrations, scouring history books, archives, newspapers and Victoria's diaries for the exact details of the couple's big day in 1840. Lucy also recounts the story of Victoria and Albert's courtship and engagement, exploring the hidden iconography and symbolism of this hugely significant wedding and revealing new insights into the pair's relationship, both public and private(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Building Cars: Secrets of the Assembly Line
programme.desc: James May, Kate Humble and Ant Anstead broadcast live from inside one of Britain's biggest and busiest car factories. In this edition, the team reveals what it takes to build thousands of different cars a day, as a car that started the manufacturing process yesterday drives off the production line for the first time(n)

programme.title: Revolutions: The Ideas That Changed the World
programme.desc: Jim Al-Khalili explores the history of robots. The first programmable machine, a self-playing trumpet, was created in 8th-century Baghdad, with the innovations of Charles Babbage, Ada Lovelace and Alan Turing paving the way for machines that can mimic human behaviour. The programme examines modern machines that can walk, manipulate things and interact with people, and considers the possibility of artificial intelligence becoming a reality(n)

programme.title: Franco Building with Jonathan Meades
programme.desc: The writer considers the architectural legacy of Francisco Franco, arguing that it is not found in the official buildings he commissioned, but in the clash of building styles along Spain's coastline and the proliferation of skyscrapers in the beach resorts such as Benidorm, where the dictator was keen to promote mass tourism, leading to the creation of a new kind of city(n)

programme.title: Jerusalem: The Making of a Holy City
programme.desc: Simon Sebag Montefiore explores how Jerusalem became a sacred place in Islam, charting Muhammad's relationship with the city, the Crusaders' attempts to reclaim it for Christianity and the construction of holy shrine the Dome of the Rock. He also profiles lesser-known characters in the religion's history, including Al Hakim and Queen Melisende, and discovers parallels between 13th-century peace talks and the current tensions in the Middle East(n)

programme.title: Art of America
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon charts the history of American art on a coast-to-coast journey, beginning in the east, where he discusses how the country's rise to power was underpinned by the destruction of Native American cultural heritage. He examines portraits of Puritan settlers in Massachusetts, tells the story of a famous painting of Quaker settler William Penn, reads one of the world's most expensive books in Philadelphia and explore the wilderness landscapes that inspired artist Thomas Cole(n)

programme.title: Bauhaus Rules with Vic Reeves
programme.desc: Six graduates from Central St Martins are challenged to create a new work of art, design or performance inspired by artists who taught at the Bauhaus. Key figures from today's worlds of art and design - who have been influenced by the Bauhaus in their own work - set the rules for the experiment. Presented by Vic Reeves(n)

programme.title: Revolutions: The Ideas That Changed the World
programme.desc: Jim Al-Khalili explores the history of robots. The first programmable machine, a self-playing trumpet, was created in 8th-century Baghdad, with the innovations of Charles Babbage, Ada Lovelace and Alan Turing paving the way for machines that can mimic human behaviour. The programme examines modern machines that can walk, manipulate things and interact with people, and considers the possibility of artificial intelligence becoming a reality(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: History of the Future: Cars
programme.desc: Phill Jupitus examines how cars of the future were predicted to look in the 1950s and 1960s. In his quest to trace the dream car of his childhood, he visits the Henry Ford Museum and the GM Heritage Centre in Detroit, as well as the Science Museum in London, with contributions from Jonathan Glancy and Clive Sinclair(n)

programme.title: A History of Scotland
programme.desc: Neil Oliver explores the importance of Gaelic to the country's past and looks at the impact of a feud between two clans on the Highlands and Lowlands. He examines how the policies of the Stewarts in the 15th century led to the Macdonalds being perceived as rebels and outsiders, and to Scots becoming the dominant language(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of Landfill: A Rubbish History
programme.desc: In a unique science experiment, Dr George McGavin and Dr Zoe Laughlin are given unprecedented access to one of the UK's largest landfill sites to investigate the history of rubbish(n)

programme.title: Susan Calman's Fringe Benefits
programme.desc: The host catches up with one-time Blue Peter presenter Janet Ellis, who has been appearing at the book festival. She also meets acting legends Arabella Weir and Cora Bissett, and comedians Nish Kumar and Mark Nelson(n)

programme.title: The Friday Documentary
programme.desc: In a documentary first aired in 1989, Mini enthusiasts around the world celebrate the famous small car's 30th birthday. Contributors, including comedian Spike Milligan and politician Tony Benn, explain the little car's unique character(n)

programme.title: Jane Austen: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Historian Lucy Worsley embarks on a road trip across England to take in some of the places Jane Austen lived in and visited during her lifetime. She aims to demonstrate how Jane's personal experiences influenced the central themes of home and property in her novels, and makes use of detective-work to recreate some of the most important locations that no longer stand. She begins in Stoneleigh Abbey, Warwickshire, retracing a journey that marked one of the many times the Austens veered wildly between wealth and genteel poverty - mirroring the Dashwoods of Sense and Sensibility. Lucy also travels from Jane's birthplace in Steventon, Hampshire, to her brother's grand estate at Godmersham Park - the inspiration for Darcy's Pemberley in Pride and Prejudice, along with Lyme Regis, Bath and Southampton(n)

programme.title: The Worst Witch
programme.desc: Everyone's surprised to hear the Great Wizard is making an unexpected visit to Cackle's Academy. He's had a tip off that the school is going rapidly downhill and it looks like non-magical Mildred is at the heart of the problems. Miss Cackle (really Agatha) refuses to expel Mildred and waxes lyrical about her. The Great Wizard insists that Mildred should perform a magical display to prove herself. Creative Mildred conducts a magical potions choir, but it ends in disaster leaving Miss Cackle on thin ice. To everyone's surprise, Miss Cackle (Agatha) makes Mildred lead a broomstick display. Mildred works out that Agatha is impersonating Miss Cackle but has to go ahead with the display - not realising Ethel had put a spell on the broomstick. Mildred begs Maud to find the real Miss Cackle, so while Mildred and Enid are in peril in the air, Maud battles her way up to Miss Cackle's room to find her under an entrancement spell. Just as Mildred and Enid start to plummet, the real Miss Cackle sav(n)

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.sub-title: Battle of the Bands - Our Lives
programme.desc: Documentary following the Cory and Tredegar brass bands as they prepare to battle it out at the Welsh Regional Championships in Wrexham. As well as looking at the history of brass bands, the film hears from members including Tim, who overcame his tearaway youth to excel at the tuba, and Rob, who postponed his kidney transplant to play in the competition(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: Police from the UK and Uganda trace a woman who fled Britain after killing her daughter, and officers in London are on the hunt for a man wanted in Italy for committing rape and serious assault. Meanwhile in Yorkshire, police search for a criminal who used a light aircraft to smuggle cocaine(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Comedian Paul Merton investigates his family history. His mother's Irish parents died when she was very young, so Paul sets out to piece together the story of his grandfather's involvement in key events leading up to Irish independence in the 1920s, and learns that the true circumstances of his death were very different from the story that had been handed down. Elsewhere in London, Paul traces a great, great grandmother on his father's side of the family who was sent to prison for her part in an assault(n)

programme.title: Scotland's Home of the Year
programme.desc: Judges Michael Angus, Anna Campbell-Jones and Kate Spiers consider properties that are situated on Skye and Arran, including a single-storey build with breathtaking views, and a custom built-home that blends the styles of the Scottish and American owners. Later, the judges complete their list of the Scotland-wide finalists for next week's final instalment of the series(n)

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland
programme.desc: Crossing the Sea of the Hebrides, Paul Murton heads to the Isle of Ewe, and onto the Summer Isles(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Whitney finds Callum's letters from Chris and questions him, but he cannot bring himself to tell her the truth. Ben spots Mel on the Square and demands information about Lisa, but she is quick to brush him off before returning to Hunter. Despite Chantelle and Keegan trying to stop her, Bailey arranges a meeting with a medium(n)

programme.title: Poldark
programme.desc: Several months have passed and Ross has got in deeper with the French invasion force as they prepare to welcome their leader General Toussaint. Meanwhile, Morwenna and Drake are ready to welcome their baby, while George stumbles on the means to undo Ross once and for all with the help of Merceron and Hanson. Ross and his friends must launch their most daring plan yet to try to save themselves and the country from enemies both at home and abroad. Last-ever episode of the drama, starring Aidan Turner(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: At Westminster, Tommy, Ada and Oswald Mosley meet to discuss business, while a rival gang keen to send a message uses brutal means to do so. Arthur and Finn reveal their plans to expand the Peaky business empire, while a birthday celebration is interrupted by the unexpected arrival of someone close to Tommy with a message about a dangerous new enemy. Cillian Murphy stars(n)

programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat, hosted by Matt Baker(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ben corners Mel and after revealing he saw her by the flat Hunter is hiding in, she relents and gives him what he wants. Jack piles the pressure on Adam to ensure the mortgage goes through quickly, and when Honey admits it might take longer than planned, Jack doesn't back down. Concerned for Mick, Linda books him a doctor's appointment despite his reluctance(n)

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Lou struggles with the revelation that she is adopted, while Maggie learns the truth about Ruby's pregnancy and Angus attempts to deter an over-enthusiastic one-night stand(n)

programme.title: Keeping Faith
programme.desc: The deal with Corran Energy draws closer to reaching completion, as Faith races against time to track down the only woman who can overturn Madlen's conviction. DI Breeze gives Evan an ultimatum, while Steve hatches a plot to rid both himself and Faith of the Reardons once and for all. Tom and Lisa are both confronted with difficult truths, and Faith finally learns the real circumstances of Will's murder. Conclusion of the Wales-set legal drama, starring Eve Myles, Aimee-Ffion Edwards and Rhashan Stone(n)

programme.title: The Royal Edinburgh Military Tattoo
programme.desc: Bill Paterson narrates highlights of the event held on the esplanade of Edinburgh Castle, while Aasmah Mir goes behind-the-scenes to meet some of the performers and get a flavour of how this enormous event comes together. The British Army is at the fore, with the bands of the Scots Guards, the Irish Guards and the Royal Regiment of Scotland, complemented by international performers including the New Zealand Army Band, the Tobago Defence Force Steel Orchestra and the Beijing Marching Wind Band and Cultural Display Team(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ben corners Mel and after revealing he saw her by the flat Hunter is hiding in, she relents and gives him what he wants. Jack piles the pressure on Adam to ensure the mortgage goes through quickly, and when Honey admits it might take longer than planned, Jack doesn't back down. Concerned for Mick, Linda books him a doctor's appointment despite his reluctance(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Phoebe is admitted to hospital, and Evan accompanies her. Chloe is left dismayed by the development, as there seems to be no hope of cutting him out of her life altogether. Essie must make a difficult decision when a former patient asks her for a drastic favour, and Ric is concerned when a member of the Trust is admitted, and places the patient in Cameron's hands(n)

programme.title: Keeping Faith
programme.desc: The deal with Corran Energy draws closer to reaching completion, as Faith races against time to track down the only woman who can overturn Madlen's conviction. DI Breeze gives Evan an ultimatum, while Steve hatches a plot to rid both himself and Faith of the Reardons once and for all. Tom and Lisa are both confronted with difficult truths, and Faith finally learns the real circumstances of Will's murder. Conclusion of the Wales-set legal drama, starring Eve Myles, Aimee-Ffion Edwards and Rhashan Stone(n)

programme.title: Reggie Yates: Extreme Russia
programme.sub-title: Reggie Yates' Extreme Russia: Far Right & Proud
programme.desc: The broadcaster travels to Moscow to meet Russia's nationalists, who idolise President Putin and threaten violence to show devotion to their country. He also talks to migrants who have been the victims of racist attacks in the city, and considers how a rise of far right groups could impact other countries, such as the UK(n)

programme.title: Walk the Line
programme.desc: Barra Best sets out to uncover and explore some of Northern Ireland's lost railways, beginning with a visit to Co Down's answer to Stonehenge. The presenter also hears about a battle to control Newcastle's growth as a tourist resort, as well as enjoying a day at the races(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: Police from the UK and Uganda trace a woman who fled Britain after killing her daughter, and officers in London are on the hunt for a man wanted in Italy for committing rape and serious assault. Meanwhile in Yorkshire, police search for a criminal who used a light aircraft to smuggle cocaine(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Comedian Paul Merton investigates his family history. His mother's Irish parents died when she was very young, so Paul sets out to piece together the story of his grandfather's involvement in key events leading up to Irish independence in the 1920s, and learns that the true circumstances of his death were very different from the story that had been handed down. Elsewhere in London, Paul traces a great, great grandmother on his father's side of the family who was sent to prison for her part in an assault(n)

programme.title: Heartbreak Holiday
programme.desc: Documentary following 10 strangers united by heartbreak as they head off for the summer of a lifetime around the Greek islands. In the final episode, emotions run high as the life-changing holiday comes to an end. The girls decide to confront Luke, and Archie and Melissa challenge themselves at sea(n)

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Cherry Healey and a team of independent scientists conduct an experiment that puts everyday cosmetic and beauty products to the test. Cherry discovers that products such a moisturisers may not have the benefits we once thought, and reveals an easy fix for cellulite that actually works. The programme also looks at the psychology behind why we buy certain cosmetic and beauty products(n)

programme.desc: Jonathan Sumption assesses the US and UK's constitutional models. He describes Britain's unwritten constitution as a political institution. programme.title: Cuimhneachan (Remembrance)
programme.desc: The legacy of the Great War through the eyes of the bards who were writing in the years after the conflict. It was thought it would be 'the war to end all wars', but it wasn't and that realisation led many poets such as Murdo MacFarlane to question the effectiveness of war in general. However, at the same time it was realised that the sacrifice of those who went into battle should never be forgotten. Featuring specially recorded performances by Gaelic singers Art Cormack, Linda MacLeod, Murdo MacDonald and Robert Robertson(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Businessman and owner of the Causton Lions rugby team Mark Adler is mysteriously found dead in the club's cryotherapy chamber, which is normally used by the players for physiotherapy. Barnaby and Winter are called in to investigate and their list of suspects soon includes Adler's wife Samantha, former coach Guy Bevan, troubled star player Danny Wickham and physio Neville Gallagher. programme.title: Fighter Pilot: The Real Top Gun
programme.desc: Documentary following three trainees at Britain's elite fighter pilot school, including the establishment's only female student. In the first episode, the pilots are put through their paces learning to fly the new F35 Lightning stealth jet. These planes can avoid radar, hover and land vertically, but with only a few months to bring them to the UK and prepare them for active service, the stakes a re high for the aspiring pilots(n)

programme.title: Deep Water
programme.desc: Lisa tries to make amends for her part in Lucinda's disappearance but is starting to see a different side to Kate and Guy. Her husband Joe tells her that she should focus on her own family, as their daughter Sally has struggles to cope with the recent events. Guy learns that Sally has been keeping information from the investigation and storms round to Lisa's home. Meanwhile, Detective Constable Joanne Aspinall starts to look into the Rivertys' family history, and grows suspicious of Guy. Starring Anna Friel, Sinead Keenan and Rosalind Eleazar(n)

programme.desc: Documentary following three trainees at Britain's elite fighter pilot school, including the establishment's only female student. In the first episode, the pilots are put through their paces learning to fly the new F35 Lightning stealth jet. These planes can avoid radar, hover and land vertically, but with only a few months to bring them to the UK and prepare them for active service, the stakes a re high for the aspiring pilots(n)

programme.title: Deep Water
programme.desc: Lisa tries to make amends for her part in Lucinda's disappearance but is starting to see a different side to Kate and Guy. Her husband Joe tells her that she should focus on her own family, as their daughter Sally has struggles to cope with the recent events. Guy learns that Sally has been keeping information from the investigation and storms round to Lisa's home. Meanwhile, Detective Constable Joanne Aspinall starts to look into the Rivertys' family history, and grows suspicious of Guy. Starring Anna Friel, Sinead Keenan and Rosalind Eleazar(n)

programme.desc: Robert has to make what could be a far-reaching decision about the recent bombshell. programme.title: Downton Abbey

programme.sub-title: Cold Is the Grave - Part Two

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A student production of The Merchant of Venice is halted when the actor playing Shylock is murdered during the performance. The detectives discover the victim had an expensive lifestyle and not only owed money to his fellow students, but was also suspected of stealing from them. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth has retired to a charming English village in the hope of finding peace and quiet. programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar return as detectives Cassie and Sunny, who find themselves thrown into another historic murder case with the discover of a body. This time it's the perfectly preserved remains of a middle-aged man found in a suitcase in the silt of a London river. As the detectives begin the complicated task of trying to identify the victim, four unconnected people go about their business - Brighton-based lawyer Colin Osborne, paediatric nurse Marion Kelsey, teacher Sara Mahmoud and respected police officer Tessa Nixon - unaware they are about to be dragged into the investigation. Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar star, with Mark Bonnar, Lorraine Ashbourne, Charlie Condou and Wendy Craig(n)

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: With their victim identified, Cassie and Sunny begin to find out a bit more about David Walker by talking to his ex-wife, DI Tessa Nixon, who reveals he suffered from depression. A search through the original investigation files and some numbers from David's pager help build a picture of him - and also lead to a few potential suspects. However, his son Jason struggles to deal with the news that someone murdered his dad. Whodunit, starring Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar, with Lorraine Ashbourne, Mark Bonnar, Douglas Hodge, Nigel Lindsay and Will Brown(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Robert has to make what could be a far-reaching decision about the recent bombshell. programme.title: Downton Abbey

programme.title: Scott & Bailey

programme.desc: The detective's latest case presents him with a baffling conundrum in the shape of two seemingly random murders, with the victims coming from very different ends of the social spectrum - one is a countess, and the other a showgirl. Rowan Atkinson makes his fourth appearance as the French detective from Georges Simenon's series of novels(n)

programme.sub-title: Cold Is the Grave - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. Meanwhile, an armed robbery in Eastvale turns into a murder investigation after the perpetrator is found dead in his home and when a battered body is found in a local nightclub, the identity of the victim leads Banks and Cabbot to a devastating realisation about both cases(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.sub-title: Cold Is the Grave - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. As Banks pushes all the suspects hard, the case threatens to undermine his integrity as an officer and brings his relationship with Cabbot to breaking point(n)

programme.title: GoldenEye
programme.desc: James Bond heads for Russia to investigate the connection between the theft of an experimental helicopter and an orbital satellite weapon with the power to devastate entire cities. He discovers both have fallen into the hands of a mysterious criminal mastermind with a grudge against Britain and a connection with 007's past. Spy adventure, with Pierce Brosnan taking on his first assignment as 007. Sean Bean, Izabella Scorupco and Famke Janssen also star. Directed by Martin Campbell, who later helmed Daniel Craig's first outing as Bond, Casino Royale(n)

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest entertainment news(n)

programme.desc: James Bond heads for Russia to investigate the connection between the theft of an experimental helicopter and an orbital satellite weapon with the power to devastate entire cities. Directed by Martin Campbell, who later helmed Daniel Craig's first outing as Bond, Casino Royale(n)

programme.title: Dirty Harry
programme.desc: Maverick San Francisco detective 'Dirty' Harry Callahan sets out to capture a deranged sniper picking off victims at random. Although his own tactics barely stay within the confines of the law, he is determined the killer will face his particular brand of justice, regardless of his superiors' disapproval. Don Siegel's thriller, starring Clint Eastwood, Harry Guardino and Andy Robinson(n)

credits.actor: Clint Eastwood, Harry Guardino, Andy Robinson

programme.title: Cycling: Vuelta a Espana
programme.desc: Action from the third stage of the Grand Tour race, which featured a 188km course from Ciudad del Juguete to Alicante. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from David Millar, reports from Daniel Friebe and commentary by Ned Boulting and David Millar(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar finds a suspicious building in Wales which is being used as a research laboratory, plunging him into the midst of a spine-chilling adventure. Spy drama, directed by and starring Roger Moore(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A mysterious criminal plants bombs around the city which cannot be disarmed. Theo's prime suspect becomes an unlikely ally when he defuses a device - but his help comes at a price(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A group of mobsters try to free their jailed boss by discrediting Quincy, whose expert testimony was responsible for putting him behind bars in the first place(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.sub-title: If Money Be the Food of Love, Play On
programme.desc: Arthur and Terry fall under the spell of a bewitching Australian beauty who has ventured from Down Under in search of her missing fiance. However, she may just happen to be concealing an ulterior motive. George Cole and Dennis Waterman star(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: The resources of CI5 are stretched when a mentally unbalanced former soldier starts killing hospital staff, believing them responsible for the death of his wife and for the brain damage suffered by his young daughter. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan is joined by an old friend - Turkish policeman Captain Shebbeq - to help him track down a gold-smuggling ring. As he observes a secret government deal to transport the precious commodity, he comes to suspect there is something rather sinister also within the cargo. Nadim Sawalha guest stars, with John Thaw(n)

programme.title: The Shawshank Redemption
programme.desc: A banker is wrongly jailed for his wife's murder and over the course of a life sentence he forms a close friendship with a fellow inmate. He also earns a position of trust as book-keeper to the corrupt prison governor - but makes an enemy of his benefactor as he tries to clear his name. Frank Darabont's drama, starring Tim Robbins, Morgan Freeman, Bob Gunton, William Sadler, Clancy Brown and Gil Bellows(n)

credits.actor: Tim Robbins, Morgan Freeman, Bob Gunton, William Sadler, Clancy Brown, Gil Bellows

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Noel Fielding, Sandi Toksvig and judges Prue Leith and Paul Hollywood return with the culinary competition, which this year features the added pressure of a 13th contestant. The series gets underway with cake week - during which two of the bakers will be sent home. The first stage of the challenge features a fruit cake, while the technical is a retro classic that requires intricate decoration. Finally, for the showstopper, the contestants must create the birthday cake they wish they had received as a child(n)

programme.title: Sink or Swim for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: A team of celebrities train for a cross-channel relay swim in support of Stand Up to Cancer, under the coaching of Olympic swimmer Keri-Anne Payne and adventurer Ross Edgley. The team consists of athletes Linford Christie, Tessa Sanderson and Greg Rutherford, Coronation Street's Sair Khan, The Last Leg's Alex Brooker, TOWIE's James Argent and Georgia Kousoulou, Blue singer Simon Webbe, Love Island's Wes Nelson, Hollyoaks actress Rachel Adedeji, and presenter Diane Louise Jordan(n)

programme.title: Kathy Burke's All Woman
programme.desc: The actress discusses the nature of compromise in a relationship, talking to Caroline Flack, who reveals how she was once tempted to give up her career altogether for the sake of her boyfriend, and Skunk Anansie singer Skin, who talks about her different experiences in relationships with both men and women. Kathy also goes on a chaste date with a gigolo, who claims to offer women freedom from having to compromise during sex, and discusses the neurochemistry of love with an Oxford academic, who stresses that friendship is equally important(n)

programme.title: Jade: The Reality Star Who Changed Britain
programme.desc: Ten years on from her death, this series explores the rollercoaster life of Jade Goody, one of the most loved - and hated - reality TV stars, to tell a bigger story of class, politics and cultural change in Britain, at a time when reality TV became the biggest and most polarising form of entertainment in the country. The concluding episode looks back at the final years of Jade's life after her diagnosis of cervical cancer in 2008, and her work to raise awareness of cervical cancer in young women(n)

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: The Naked Chef Bares All
programme.desc: Presented by Davina McCall, this documentary charts the life of the celebrity chef, looking back at memorable moments throughout his career: his accidental discovery at The River Café, his rise to fame as iconic an Brit-pop chef, his unwavering campaigning to improve the food system, and the more recent closure of his Jamie's Italian restaurant group. As well as his career, the programme also features exclusive access to Jamie's private family life, uncovering emotional revelations and personal insights surrounding the two decades he has spent in the spotlight(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Holiday Resort
programme.desc: Another look at life at an all-inclusive Holiday World Resort in Malaga, Spain, to see what Britons get up to while on holiday in the sun and what it takes to keep them happy. Liverpudlian friends Paddy, Steve and John take time out from their families to take advantage of the all-inclusive bar and the full range of the evening entertainment the resort has on offer(n)

programme.title: The Secret Teacher
programme.desc: Despite being expelled from school at 15, Steven Bartlett runs one of the biggest social media marketing companies in the world, turning over millions of pounds a year. Now he goes undercover at Litherland, a cash-strapped school in Liverpool. During his six weeks, Steven meets Eloise, whose casual attitude hides a difficult past, and Ste, who dreams of becoming a millionaire. Steven also wants to use his own experience to help improve the mental health of staff and students, but how will headteacher Mr Rodgers respond to the newcomer's ideas?(n)

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Dan Duffy, CEO of United Real Estate Group, adopts a disguise to go undercover in his own company. United has spent a lot of money on technology platforms, so he feels it is imperative to make sure each department is utilising all the programs and that they are working as designed. Dan meets a range of different employees to learn about both their professional standards and their personal lives(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs New Zealand
programme.desc: Chris Moller meets ambitious and innovative people who are creating their dream homes. programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Contestants on Spain's Costa del Sol try to outdo one another by throwing the perfect dinner party. First to host is Michelle, an Abba tribute band singer who hopes to be the winner who takes it all. Her healthy meal wins over her guests, but flamboyant bar owner Charles says it like it is, causing hilarity and offence in equal measure - much to the delight of self-confessed cougar Kate. Super chilled-out DJ Ryan is the fourth of the contenders hoping to secure the £1,000 prize on offer(n)

Former staff reveal how Aldi has overcome shoppers' suspicions using a host of clever techniques to cut costs, from multiple barcodes and less time spent at the tills, to their own-brand products - which are intended to look and taste like their big-name counterparts, but cost a fraction of the price(n)

programme.title: Concorde
programme.desc: First of a two-part documentary in which aircraft designers, pilots and passengers tell the story of how the Anglo-French Concorde saw off competition from the Americans and Soviets to become the world's first supersonic passenger airliner. Michael Heseltine describes how the Americans made it impossible for Concorde to be sold to any other airlines other than the national carriers of Britain and France, and even went so far as to try and ban it from the route it had been designed for— London to New York(n)

programme.title: The Yorkshire Vet

A pygmy goat keeps getting its head stuck in a wire fence, a dog needs an operation on its lungs, and farmers the Greens celebrate their anniversary with a trip to Whitby(n)

programme.title: Inside the Tower of London
programme.desc: The chief yeoman warder oversees the tower's celebration of the end of the First World War, and special preparations are made for the tower director's retirement party, involving a military band and a unique farewell as he leaves the tower for the last time. Newly hatched raven George is introduced to the rest of the birds, not all of whom take kindly to newcomers, and historian Tracy Borman unearths the story of Roger Casement, who was executed for treason for supporting Irish independence during the First World War(n)

programme.title: The Great Fire
programme.sub-title: The Great Fire of London: Hour by Hour
programme.desc: Dan Jones, Suzannah Lipscomb and Rob Bell take viewers back to one of the most momentous events in English history - the devastating fire of September 1666 that obliterated almost every building inside the city walls of medieval London. They follow the blaze as it rampaged out of control, to reveal what actually happened, hour by hour, and street by street(n)

Firstly, they must craft two batches of perfectly uniform miniature classics - Linzer tortes and Bakewell tarts - in three and a half hours. programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Stephen Hammon hosts the third day of the competition at the four-star Old Hall House B&B in Warwickshire, providing his guests with a variety of on-site activities including shooting and archery before taking them for a meal at his own restaurant. However, he ends up trying too hard to impress at breakfast the next day after Sandie De Rougemont reveals her past as a hotel inspector(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Ex-hotel inspector Sandie De Rougemont, owner of the House of Agnes in Canterbury, plays host. She takes her guests to a local brewery and an upmarket restaurant, where everyone seems to enjoy themselves, but the revelation that a ghost may roam the rooms of the B&B leaves some of the participants nervous(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the week's competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table(n)

programme.title: Ugly House to Lovely House with George Clarke
programme.desc: George is joined by leading architects to help transform some of Britain's most unloved houses into desirable properties, beginning with the 1970s home of Tony and Sarah in the Vale of Glamorgan. The couple want rid of the pebbledash facade, small windows and dated decor, to create a great family home on a relatively modest budget of £60,000. They receive some bespoke and cutting-edge input from commercial and residential architect Greg Blee, with a plan that will link their home to the landscape beyond, including a Shard-like extension(n)

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.desc: Joan Bakewell and Stephen Mangan launch another talent search, with the first nine contestants being given four hours to capture the likeness of celebrity sitters Matthew Goode, Geraldine James and Andi Oliver(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A small cavachon is rushed to see Professor Noel Fitzpatrick after being hit by a car. She has a broken leg, but also a serious heart condition. Meanwhile, Simba, a one-year-old Labrador needs help with her badly formed hips, and Tulee the nervous basset hound has diseased discs in her back that leave her in constant pain(n)

programme.title: The Best Exotic Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: Seven English pensioners looking for a fresh start are drawn to an advert for a hotel in the Indian city of Jaipur, and plan to spend their retirement there. They arrive to find the building dilapidated but are won over by the enthusiastic young manager, and each embarks on their own adventures in the city. Comedy drama, starring Judi Dench, Bill Nighy, Maggie Smith and Dev Patel(n)

credits.actor: Judi Dench, Bill Nighy, Maggie Smith, Dev Patel

programme.title: The World's Most Beautiful Railway
programme.desc: Documentary about Scotland's railway network, meeting people who work on it as well as the steam enthusiasts who safeguard these famous routes. In the first edition, 200 lucky passengers board A4 steam locomotive Union of South Africa as it makes a farewell tour after 50 years of service(n)

programme.title: Coastal Railways with Julie Walters
programme.desc: The actress travels around the UK coast by train, beginning by boarding the famous Jacobite steam train for a trip along Scotland's West Highland Railway, famously featured in the Harry Potter movies. Along the way, Julie learns about herring gutting and blowing up railway lines, and meets a traditional Scottish storyteller on the Isle of Skye(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts, with Sean Lock and Stephen Mangan taking on guest captain Richard Ayoade and Katherine Ryan. Bill Bailey is in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent, while Rachel Riley is in charge of the letters and numbers(n)

programme.title: Shocking Emergency Calls
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of the day a gunman entered a bank in Cincinnati and opened fire, featuring the calls made by those caught up in the atrocity(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Documentary following life inside one of Britain's busiest A&E departments at King's College Hospital in south London. A doctor is shocked when a young woman arrives in resus with life-threatening swelling on her brain following a random attack by a stranger on the streets, while a mother faces an agonising wait as tests are carried out on her 12-year-old son, who was hit by a car and airlifted to King's by emergency medics. A 90-year-old former circus performer is also in A&E after collapsing at home, and news of his colourful past quickly circulates among the staff(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A small cavachon is rushed to see Professor Noel Fitzpatrick after being hit by a car. She has a broken leg, but also a serious heart condition. Meanwhile, Simba, a one-year-old Labrador needs help with her badly formed hips, and Tulee the nervous basset hound has diseased discs in her back that leave her in constant pain(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty investigates supermarkets' stringent criteria for the perfect vegetable, and the incredible lengths to which farmers go to grow produce that looks more attractive. In America, Kate Quiton examines why wine is getting stronger and learns about a machine that has been removing excess alcohol from some Californian wines since the 1990s. Matt Tebbutt takes on the ultimate tea-break challenge as he tries to settle an age-old dispute once and for all: which biscuit is best for dunking?(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Fill Your House for Free
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp comes to the rescue of Dave and Kate, whose house has been flooded(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty investigates a new technology developed in Ireland that uses high pressure to force crabs, lobsters and mussels to pop clean out of their shells. Kate Quilton learns why toddlers are hardwired to avoid unfamiliar foods and examines techniques to get fussy eaters to eat vegetables, while Matt Tebbutt heads to Germany to find out how gummy sweets are made(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Billy, Gwendoline and Michael finally hope to move into their chateau so they can host a hen party, although the kitchen's unfinished. In the Loire Valley, Paul and Karen rent out their chateau as a holiday gite hoping to attract more guests by creating an office in an old water tower. Meanwhile, Dick and Angel try to keep costs down when shopping for baths(n)

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: The five remaining teams of professional pastry chefs face the scrutiny of judges Cherish Finden and Benoit Blin. Participants have to create 24 layered fruit cake slices and 24 lemon-shaped desserts. For their final challenge, contestants have five hours to create an éclair tower to serve 48 people. Liam Charles and Tom Allen are the hosts(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole restores two quad bikes and a stylish sun lounger, while Simon O'Brien rescues a holed canoe and turns an old oak beam into a spice rack(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: A quartet of B&B owners battle it out to decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money, beginning with luxury weight-loss and detox retreat Slimmeria in East Sussex. The guests worry about uncomfortable sleeping arrangements, the price of accommodation and the strict 9pm curfew - and the prospect of a dance-style workout only exacerbates their bad mood(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jan and William Cheeseman, owners of five-star Gold-rated 38 St Giles in Norwich, try to impress their competitors. Unfortunately, poor breakfast presentation and complaints about noise from the street increase the tension between the couple as they strive for perfection(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Ann and Bill Clay play host at their four-star Bosvean House B&B in Bude, Cornwall. Diane and Max Seivewright query the establishment's luxury description, while Galia Grainger complains about the lack of a sea view. However, the hosts hope an indulgent Cornish cream tea, a stick dance and a drink in their garden shed will succeed in winning over the guests(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Village bakers Diane and Max Seivewright, the owners of Ombersley B&B in Worcestershire, host the last round of the competition, but some guests are far from impressed with the rooms and food(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners gather to find out who has won the competition. Diane and Max Seivewright, the hosts at Ombersley B&B, feel betrayed by Ann and Bill Clay's feedback and want to know why they have been so harshly criticised, while Galia Grainger asks why Slimmeria's rules and regulations have provoked so much hostility(n)

programme.title: Ugly House to Lovely House with George Clarke
programme.desc: Architect Ed Lipton renovates a house for a couple who moved in nine years ago for £220,000, but with two young sons are fast outgrowing the property and cannot afford to move. Ed's bold ideas are held up by planning problems and once the build starts, Rachel and James agree to move out so progress can be quicker, but it has to be completed in just eight weeks. Knocking down the garage and replacing it with a dramatic, double-height extension to give the living space a real sense of drama is one of the potential solutions, although the budget of £60,000 is set to test Ed's skill to the limit(n)

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Pedro the giant anteater is about to be reunited with long-term mate Bliss, but falls ill and faces a major operation, with an uncertain outcome. Chester Zoo's extremely rare bald ibises produce a batch of new chicks, which are destined to be released back into the wild in southern Spain. There is also a chance to witness the reunion of rhino couple Beni and Asha, who have been kept separate for four years(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick offers cutting-edge treatment for Luca, a long-suffering four-year-old springer spaniel with a painful elbow disease. He designs and creates a special screw for Luca's leg with a system for injecting a bone-growth gel around it, and after an eight-year-old Newfoundland collapses, its owners face an agonising wait for the MRI scans to reveal if anything can be done for the gentle giant(n)

programme.title: Ugly House to Lovely House with George Clarke
programme.desc: Sue and Simon bought their four-bedroom Hertfordshire home in 2014, but the fake-Victorian extension has given the property a bad layout which is proving something of a nightmare. The couple also believe their home is by far the ugliest on their street. Enter architect Laura Jane Clark, who specialises in breathing new life into unappealing spaces. She has a plan to entirely transform the interior by moving the staircase, putting in skylights and installing an innovative revolving wall. However, Sue and Simon have a tight budget of £50,000 - and that's before they've even started on the exterior(n)

programme.desc: At Chester Zoo, Rothschild giraffe Tula is a week overdue after a 13-month pregnancy. But keepers are anxious that history could repeat itself - she gave birth to a stillborn calf last year. Cameras also follow this year's brood of penguin chicks. They are about to face the ordeal of joining the main pool, where they must compete for fish with the adult penguins. And baby silvery gibbon Eko learns how to swing between ropes - 50 feet up(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Rescue Labrador Tom needs work with a serious wound on one of his front paws, and four-year-old French bulldog called Otis has suffered an accident while being chased by another dog. programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.sub-title: Live at the Apollo Christmas Special
programme.desc: The BAFTA nominated comedy showcase returns for it's third annual Christmas special, welcoming some of the biggest and best comedic talent to the stage for some Yuletide frolics. Previous editions have featured stand-up routines and compering from renowned comedians such as Ed Byrne, Nish Kumar, Rob Beckett, Sara Pascoe and Henning Wehn. This time, Canadian comedian, writer and actress Katherine Ryan hosts the show, welcoming former I'm A Celebrity contestant and stand-up comic Joel Dommett, and Bristol-born comedian and radio presenter John Robins to the stage(n)

programme.title: Jon Richardson: Ultimate Worrier

programme.title: Raiders of the Lost Past
